summary.scholar: On January 1, 1863, President Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation, declaring enslaved people in Confederate areas forever free. It transformed the war from a struggle to preserve the Union into a battle for human freedom.
summary.explorer: On New Years Day 1863, President Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation, which said that enslaved people in the Confederate states were now free.

significance.scholar: The proclamation opened Union military service to African Americans. Nearly 179,000 Black soldiers served in the Union Army by wars end.
significance.explorer: After this, about 179,000 Black soldiers joined the Union Army to fight for freedom.
